channel.title: New local problems on FixMyStreet
channel.description: The latest local problems reported by users

item.title: Two out of four lights not working (23/11/2025, 7th April
item.description:
Replace defective lights of the asset VIR 45 with OS Ref: TQ36296988.

Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): Kings Hall Road, Penge, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Neighbours leaving rubbish, 7th April
item.description:
Foxes ripping bags open

Nearest address to the pin placed on the map (from OS Places API): William Barefoot Drive, Eltham
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Rubbish bin outside cafe, 7th April
item.description:
Good morning I would like to find out of it would be possible to have the bin moved from directly outside my cafe seating area. The bin is constantly full of bags of dog poo and many dog walkers allow their dogs to wee up the bin. We have a hygeine rating of 5 which is very important to us and as you can imagine the bin is very un-hygenic and not pleasant in the warm weather when customers are consuming food and drinks in the outside area. I apologise if i have not contacted the correct dept but i was unsure who i should be contacting. Kind regards Louise Kisi

Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): 21, Montpelier Avenue, Albany Park, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Graffiti on planters in Concert Hall Approach, 7th April
item.description:
As above

Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): Concert Hall Approach, Waterloo, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Flytipping of black bags on the junction of St Augustines Road and Upper Abbey Road, 7th April
item.description:
Flytipping of black bags on the junction of St Augustines Road and Upper Abbey Road - has been reported previously under ref; 9224295 and still not been collected. Now foxes are ripping open the bags and there's a horrible smell now - can these be collected ASAP!
Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): Upper Abbey Road, Lower Belvedere, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Dumped cardboard tubes, 7th April
item.description:
As above

Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): Leroy Street, Walworth, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Litter strewn around pub bins as ususl, 7th April
item.description:
As above

Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): Leroy Street, Walworth, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Drain hole with no cover, 7th April
item.description:
This has been like this for over a year
Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): Cavell Road, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Hole beside path, 7th April
item.description:
Coming from St Paul's U/G, into the garden, as the second path branches off to the right and the other path goes round the east end of St Paul's there is an unnecessary hole in the left corner, difficult to see when path is busy and visitor distracted, at the end of a ditch but not leading to a drain, corner could be filled in to prevent a fall
Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): St Paul's Churchyard, City of London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: 134 Brondesbury Road, 7th April
item.description:
Litter/cigarette buds that needs to be sweeped please. it's the case pretty much every week.

Nearest address to the pin placed on the map (from OS Places API): Salusbury Road, London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: This is Rubbish !, 7th April
item.description:
Overflowing bin once again. This is an ongoing issue and needs to be addressed. Bin outside Tesco Express is pretty much always full and overflowing, resulting in rubbish blowing all along the road and surrounding area. People should take rubbish home with them if bin is full, but that is not the case. Can Tesco or council provide better bin service please. We will have rats moving in before long.

Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): 2, Stone Park Avenue, Beckenham, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Big flytip by bridge, 7th April
item.description:
Bed and other items dumped next to the railway bridge. Note there's also a bag of flytipped building waste on the other side of Green Lane, too.

Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): Green Lane, Penge, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: The vehicle has ben sat there untouched for more than a year, 7th April
item.description:
The vehicle has been parked there for more than a year . it didn’t move at all. the worst thing is that it’s parked directly in front of someone’s gate on the opposite side making it very difficult for them to park cars in their own yard i’d presume.
Nearest address to the pin placed on the map (from OS Places API): Well Hall Road, Eltham
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Old furniture, 7th April
item.description:
5/4/2026 Hooks Close unwanted things on the pavement. This is probably some kind of furniture that not only litters, but is a hazard for pedestrians and especially children. Please remove the soonest possible.

Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): Cossall Walk, Peckham, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Food waste, 7th April
item.description:
A bag of rubbish was dumped here and ripped open by foxes. I reported it a few days ago and most of it was tidied up. Inexplicably this amount of food was left from the original mess.

Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): Parish Lane, Penge, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Cones blocking the highway, 7th April
item.description:
Someone near 105 Coniston Road seem to think they own the road outside their house, by installing traffic cones whenever they leave the house in their vehicle. Parking is limited due to the hotel opposite, plus they have a large driveway so do not even need the space outside their house.
Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): Coniston Road, Shortlands, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Dock/basin wall - graffiti almost the full length, 7th April
item.description:
Graffiti all over inner walls of dock/basin.

Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): Whinchat Road, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Overhang tree, 7th April
item.description:
Fallen tree overhanging in carriageway

Nearest address to the pin placed on the map (from OS Places API): Goldfinch Road, Thamesmead
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Southlands Road bus stop BV bin left full and over flowing AS USUAL at the Easter shut down., 7th April
item.description:
Southlands Road bus stop BV bin left full and over flowing AS USUAL at the Easter shut down.
Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): Bromley Common, Widmore, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Blocked river due to concrete from path, 7th April
item.description:
The river that runs alongside the pathway from Hayes to Bromley Common has been blocked at this junction by two concrete blocks and therefore does not drain correctly and also floods the path unnecessarily. These need to be reinstalled to allow the flow of water.
Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): Rookery Lane, Bromley Common, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet
